reasoning: The IBEX 35 needed to close 2025 above 11,595.00 points to resolve YES. Accounting for the typical ~3-4% dividend yield that might reduce the price index value throughout the rest of the year, the required drop to fall below 11,595 by December 31 is still a massive 11-12% relative to expected values. Standard options pricing models and historical index volatility (assume ~18-20% annualized volatility and standard positive drift) place the probability of the index staying above this heavily discounted threshold at roughly 83-87%. Given the community was at 0.75 when the index was slightly lower (~12,927) on April 17, the recent ~300+ point gains easily push the true likelihood into the low-mid 80s. Concluding with a prediction of 84% to account for broad market tail risks while acknowledging the massive buffer.
